name: bench-slack
description: Relay messages through BenchAGI's Slack relay infrastructure. Wraps sessions.create (open new thread) and sessions.send (send on cached sessionKey). All calls carry the user's Bench UID; relay enforces per-tenant Slack bot token.

tools.name: slack_session_create
tools.description: Open a new Slack relay session: resolves the recipient (Bench UID or email), picks the right channel/DM, posts the first message, and returns a sessionKey for follow-ups in the same thread.

body_schema.message: string (first message text; supports Slack mrkdwn)

tools.name: slack_session_send
tools.description: Send a follow-up message on an existing sessionKey (same thread). sessionKey TTL is 1 hour; expired keys return a specific error so callers know to open a new session.

tools.name: slack_history
tools.description: Read recent messages in a thread the caller has relay access to. Does not read arbitrary channel history — only threads the current user's relay session is authorized for.
